validation_gates.reason: Repository, immutable base, target blob, two-file change budget, review-branch route, and non-goals were resolved before mutation.

Existing packages/redaction/src placement and document identity were retained. Directory Rules, parent/source/namespace boundaries, open PRs, and matching branches were checked; no parallel authority or ADR trigger was introduced.
Main remained at 144d10ff4ac87dc37566ad608fe6b002f9250d1a from preparation through branch creation.

After the two scoped writes, main advanced to b57da43e20486de87b9b1e3e90eb8f22611658fc through policy/data/README.md and its generated receipt only. The old-base-to-new-base comparison showed no overlap with the target, linked redaction authority files, or validation configuration, so the review branch remains valid without force-updating history.

Direct reads confirmed kfm-redaction version 0.0.0, incomplete subpackage metadata, a merged namespace README v1.1, an empty __init__.py, and a comment-only core.py. Bounded search established no functional consumer import or package-test reference.

The README adds source admission, package/source/namespace layering, packaging, allowed/excluded source classes, profile activation, determinism, dependency direction, threat model, supply chain, resource limits, telemetry, consumer, test matrix, implementation sequence, definition of done, verification, compatibility, correction, and rollback contracts.

The final README has one H1, 64 headings with no hierarchy jumps or duplicate slugs, 65 resolved internal links, balanced code fences, no trailing whitespace, a parseable KFM Meta Block, and a final newline.
All nineteen repository-relative Markdown links resolve to files verified at the pinned base.
The change includes no credentials, restricted source payloads, real living-person or genomic data, protected coordinates, transform secrets, or release/publication action.
GitHub returned README blob a8dcc3b2006414046487fcb82f8264af7d08c555 on commit 63ddb7293ed61ff39c0fdfa54b2283726e29c63a, matching the locally recomputed Git blob SHA and SHA-256 artifact hash.

The connector exposes serialized Contents API writes rather than an expected-head atomic multi-file operation with readable base-tree metadata. The README and receipt are written sequentially on a scoped branch with remote verification.
The redaction package remains a greenfield placeholder and the API-only workflow did not clone or execute repository code. Pull-request checks are observed separately.
Human review remains pending and separate from generation and static validation.
